raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "Making an assertive claim about personal agency and the limits of external influence on one's circumstances",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "A person's condition is capable of being altered",
    "Only the individual himself possesses the power to make that alteration",
    "No other person or external factor can effect such a change"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "Internally coherent as a concise assertion of self-determination, though the archaic phrasing does not affect logical consistency",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "The statement is useful when the goal is to emphasize personal responsibility, encourage self-initiated change, or counter excessive external blame in motivational, therapeutic, or philosophical contexts",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "The statement is misleading when external factors (systemic barriers, others' actions, random events, or health conditions) demonstrably can and do alter a person's situation independently of their own efforts"
}
